meta.generator: SpeedyCAT BBLS AI-POC deterministic fact-bank generator
meta.disclaimer: AI-generated proof-of-concept questions. NOT real MCAT/AAMC content. Text-only (no HTML/SVG). Each item has exactly one correct answer and three distinct distractors; all items passed the 4-criterion automated eval.

questions.stem: An enzyme works best at pH 7 but loses activity at pH 2. The most likely reason is that low pH:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Increases the enzyme's molecular weight

choices.label: B
choices.text: Adds new amino acids to the enzyme

choices.label: C
choices.text: Alters charges and disrupts the active site's shape

choices.label: D
choices.text: Converts the enzyme into DNA

questions.explanation: Extreme pH changes protonation of side chains, disrupting ionic and hydrogen bonds that maintain the active site, which lowers activity.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content
questions.notes: AI-generated proof-of-concept item (text-only). Not real MCAT/AAMC content.

questions.stem: In bacterial genetics, what is transformation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: a plasmid that lets a cell act as a donor during conjugation

choices.label: B
choices.text: the transfer of bacterial DNA between cells by a bacteriophage

choices.label: C
choices.text: a mobile genetic element that can move to new positions in the genome

choices.label: D
choices.text: the uptake of free DNA from the surrounding environment

questions.explanation: transformation is the uptake of free DNA from the surrounding environment.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content
questions.notes: AI-generated proof-of-concept item (text-only). Not real MCAT/AAMC content.

questions.stem: Which feature is characteristic of incomplete dominance?

choices.label: A
choices.text: unaffected carrier parents can have affected children

choices.label: B
choices.text: males are affected far more often than females, with no male-to-male transmission

choices.label: C
choices.text: the trait passes only from fathers to all of their sons

choices.label: D
choices.text: the heterozygote shows a blended, intermediate phenotype

questions.explanation: In incomplete dominance, the heterozygote shows a blended, intermediate phenotype

questions.stem: In microbial nutrition, what is a photoautotroph?

choices.label: A
choices.text: the oxygen-requiring pathway that fully oxidizes glucose for maximal ATP

choices.label: B
choices.text: the anaerobic breakdown of sugars that regenerates NAD+ without an electron transport chain

choices.label: C
choices.text: respiration using a final electron acceptor other than oxygen

choices.label: D
choices.text: an organism using light for energy and carbon dioxide as its carbon source

questions.explanation: a photoautotroph is an organism using light for energy and carbon dioxide as its carbon source.

questions.stem: In a population at Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium, the recessive phenotype occurs in 1 out of 100 individuals. What is the frequency of the recessive allele (q)?
questions.explanation: The recessive phenotype frequency equals q-squared = 0.01, so q is the square root, 0.1.
